Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1 - Search emails for Kovacs-related correspondence using search_emails with queries such as "Kovacs" or "Toronto" or "Nashville" (or similar). Discovers Fatimah's own sent emails: (a) a detailed visa and customs analysis sent to catalina.dubois@moveops.com CC chloe.vance@moveops.com outlining TN visa departure requirements, employer letter needs, BSF186 personal effects declaration, and BorderEase Customs engagement plan; (b) a welcome email sent to renata.kovacs@gmail.com CC catalina.dubois@moveops.com with BSF186 inventory preparation instructions. Also discovers Chloe's assignment briefing email to Fatimah (CC Catalina) with move details, and Catalina's full relocation plan email to patricia.langford@canopyhealth.io mentioning surcharges pending client approval.
OE 2 - Search emails for any reply from Dr. Kovacs using search_emails with queries such as "renata.kovacs" or "Kovacs inventory" or "BSF186" (or similar). Finds NO response from renata.kovacs@gmail.com to Fatimah's inventory preparation request, confirming the BSF186 itemized list was never returned.
OE 3 - Search emails for BorderEase Customs communications using search_emails with queries such as "BorderEase" or "customs broker" (or similar). Finds references to BorderEase within other internal emails (Fatimah's analysis, Chloe's briefing, CRM engagements) but NO direct email to or from BorderEase Customs, confirming the broker was never formally engaged via email.
OE 4 - Search emails for employer letter confirmation from Patricia Langford using search_emails with queries such as "employer letter" or "Patricia" (or similar). Finds Patricia's original request emails and Catalina's plan referencing the employer letter deadline of April 7, but NO confirmation that the letter was actually sent or received. The April 7 deadline has passed with no documented follow-up.
OE 5 - Search Slack for Kovacs discussions using conversations_search_messages with queries such as "Kovacs" or "Toronto" or "customs" or "BSF186" (or similar), or retrieve history of channel C006 (#operations) using conversations_history. Discovers: (a) Fatimah's customs and visa complexity summary stating "Reaching out to BorderEase today" and asking "@chloe.vance -- can you confirm whether Swift can handle Nashville to Toronto with a border crossing? Need a yes/no before I finalize vendor" with no visible response from Chloe confirming Swift's cross-border capability; (b) Chloe's coordinator assignment post confirming Fatimah assigned to Kovacs; (c) Chloe's Airtable update post; (d) Hana's invoice breakdown post (INV-2026-0401, $9,210, with $850 customs broker surcharge flagged as pending). Also discovers Chloe's later message (approximately April 21) assigning Fatimah to the Dr. Mehta executive relocation, which references the Kovacs move as completed with "zero hiccups" -- indicating the physical move happened but records were never closed out.
OE 6 - Search or list Airtable relocation records for Kovacs using airtable_search_records or airtable_list_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table name Relocations (table ID tblRelocations01). Discovers record recKovacsNashvilleToronto: Name "Dr. Renata Kovacs", Company "Canopy Health", Status "In Progress" (should be updated since move dates April 14-17 have passed), Origin "Nashville, TN", Destination "Toronto, ON", Assigned Coordinator "Fatimah Al-Rashidi", Special Requirements listing all documentation needs (employer letter, degree verification, BorderEase Customs, BSF186, MacBook Pro declaration, lab notebooks).
OE 7 - Search CRM for the Canopy Health company record and the Kovacs deal using crm_search_companies with query "Canopy Health" (or similar) and crm_search_deals with company_id or dealname. Discovers deal_canopy_kovacs_apr2026: amount $3,850, dealstage "inprogress", description listing all complications (international move, TN visa, customs broker, employer letter deadline April 7). Also search CRM contacts using crm_search_contacts with query "Renata Kovacs" (or similar) and discovers CRM contact for Dr. Renata Kovacs (contact_canopy_renata_kovacs) still showing city as "Nashville" rather than "Toronto" -- the contact was never updated after the move.
OE 8 - List CRM engagements for Canopy Health using crm_list_engagements with company_ids including the Canopy company ID. Discovers engagement notes including: (a) coordinator assignments finalized by Chloe on April 2 with Fatimah assigned to Kovacs; (b) Fatimah's visa and customs review findings; (c) Catalina's Q2 wave CRM update noting $850 customs broker surcharge as "PENDING CLIENT APPROVAL"; (d) surcharge approval flag noting $2,650 total across three moves requires explicit Patricia sign-off before vendor commitments can be finalized.
OE 9 - Search QuickBooks for Canopy Health invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices or quickbooks_search_customers to find the customer record and related invoices. Discovers Invoice 1009 (INV-2026-0401): $9,210 total, due May 4, 2026, including line items for apartment sourcing ($3,000), flight booking ($60), moving coordination ($1,500), stipend platform ($2,000), customs broker surcharge ($850), cold-chain freight surcharge ($1,200), and temp storage surcharge ($600). The $850 customs broker surcharge line item corresponds to the Kovacs move.
OE 10 - Search Linear for any Kovacs-related issues or tickets using linear_list_issues with query "Kovacs" or project proj_canopy (or similar). Discovers ZERO existing Linear issues tracking the Kovacs relocation workstreams (visa documentation, customs broker engagement, employer letter, BSF186 filing, mover confirmation). This is a critical gap -- the most complex international move MoveOps has handled has no tracking tickets.
OE 11 - Search emails for a UK relocation vendor using search_emails with queries such as "UK relocation" or "international partner" or "Meridian" or "United Kingdom" (or similar). Discovers: (a) a cold outreach email from Asha Patel (partnerships@meridianglobalmobility.co.uk), Partnerships Director at Meridian Global Mobility (London), sent to david.chen@moveops.com in January 2026, offering UK-to-US relocation partnership services including customs coordination and household goods shipping; (b) David Chen initially forwarded this email to mina.hashimoto@moveops.com with a note saying "No action needed on my side unless something comes up"; (c) David Chen later sent a follow-up reply to Meridian (from david.chen@moveops.com, CC mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, chloe.vance@moveops.com, and fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com) referencing a live client case involving a London-to-Boston relocation and requesting a call. The original January 15 cold email sat unactioned for over 3 months before David resurfaced it. No reply from Meridian has been received.
OE 12 - Search contacts for the Meridian or UK vendor contact using contacts_search_contacts with query "Meridian" or "Asha" (or similar). Discovers contact record for Asha Patel: email asha.patel@meridianglobalmobility.co.uk, title "Partnerships Director", based in London, United Kingdom.
OE 13 - Search emails for BrightLoop international move context using search_emails with queries such as "Oliver Bancroft" or "BrightLoop international" or "London" (or similar). Discovers that BrightLoop Analytics needs to relocate Oliver Bancroft from London to Boston, and finds Fatimah's own analysis (sent as an email to chloe.vance@moveops.com, mina.hashimoto@moveops.com, and david.chen@moveops.com, and also posted as a Linear comment on the BrightLoop account audit issue) flagging that: TN visa does not apply to UK citizens, H-1B is employer-driven and timing-sensitive, MoveOps lacks an in-house UK origin or household goods export workflow, and a specialist partner is needed for export inventory and customs docs. This confirms Meridian Global Mobility (UK-to-US partner) would be relevant for the BrightLoop/Oliver situation but NOT for the Kovacs US-to-Canada customs work.
OE 14 - Look up contact details for Catalina Dubois and Chloe Vance using contacts_search_contacts with queries "Catalina" and "Chloe" (or similar). Confirms catalina.dubois@moveops.com and chloe.vance@moveops.com for the notification emails and Slack message.
OE 15 - Update the Kovacs Airtable relocation record using airtable_update_records on base appMoveOpsOps001, table tblRelocations01, record recKovacsNashvilleToronto. Update Status from "In Progress" to "Completed" and add notes to the Special Requirements field documenting the close-out audit findings: physical move completed per Chloe's approximately April 21 confirmation, but documentation items (customs broker engagement, employer letter receipt, Dr. Kovacs inventory submission, mover cross-border confirmation, surcharge approval) remain unverified.
OE 16 - Create one or more Linear issues for unresolved Kovacs documentation gaps using linear_create_issue on team team_operations or team_relocations, project proj_canopy. Title should reference Kovacs close-out documentation gaps. Description should list unconfirmed items: (1) BorderEase Customs broker engagement never confirmed via email; (2) employer letter from Canopy Health not confirmed received (April 7 deadline passed); (3) Dr. Kovacs BSF186 inventory list not received; (4) Swift Relocations cross-border capability not confirmed by Chloe; (5) $850 customs broker surcharge still pending client approval from Patricia Langford.
OE 17 - Send an email from fatimah.al-rashidi@moveops.com to catalina.dubois@moveops.com using send_email. Content should summarize the Kovacs close-out audit: the physical move was completed but documentation items remain unverified, list the specific gaps (broker engagement, employer letter, inventory, mover confirmation, surcharge approval status), flag that Patricia's approval on the $850 surcharge is still pending, note that a Linear ticket has been created, and flag that the CRM contact for Dr. Kovacs still shows Nashville and may need updating. Also mention the Meridian Global Mobility contact as potentially relevant for BrightLoop's upcoming international move.
OE 18 - Post a message in channel C006 (#operations) using conversations_add_message. Content should alert Chloe to the Kovacs close-out findings: records have been updated, documentation gaps have been ticketed, and Catalina has been notified. Flag the unresolved items and the pending surcharge approval. Optionally mention the Meridian Global Mobility lead for international capability building.
OE 19 - Log the close-out audit findings as a CRM engagement on the Canopy Health account using crm_create_engagement with engagement_type "NOTE" and company_ids including the Canopy Health company ID. Body should document: Kovacs Nashville-to-Toronto relocation physically completed April 14-17 but documentation close-out was delayed, list of unverified items, surcharge approval status, Linear ticket created for follow-up, and note about the Meridian Global Mobility partnership lead for future international moves.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
